assumptions: Browser has p5.js 1.11.2+ from CDN (no offline mode)
User will edit CONFIG in the HTML source and reload (no UI controls)
Recording mode uses JSZip and browser download API (not Node.js)
System has ffmpeg on PATH for export-gif.sh (optional; skipped if not present)

core_loop.path: index.html lines 200-400 (the p5.draw function + glyph sampling loop)
core_loop.role: Walk an N×M grid over source Graphics buffer, sample brightness/color per cell, pick character from ramp, render at source hue.

inputs: CONFIG object (canvas size, grid density, scene name, palette, sampling strategy)
p5.js draw loop frame callback at 30 FPS
Scene function returns offscreen p5.Graphics with rendered geometry
Recording mode: frame number → capture PNG
outputs: Visible canvas with character-grid composited on top of geometric source
Optional ZIP of PNG frames (glyph-grid_*.zip) when CONFIG.record=true
FFmpeg-compatible frame sequence for GIF/video export

decisions.title: Implemented brute-force shape-atlas lookup before k-d tree optimization
decisions.why: At n≈256 shapes, k=6 dims: tight Float32Array loop often equals or beats pointer-chasing k-d tree in V8. Profile first, tree second.

decisions.title: Rec.709 weights for linearized luminance, not Rec.601
decisions.why: Rec.601 weights are gamma-encoded; Rec.709 applies to linear-light sRGB after EOTF. Category error caught in code review.

decisions.title: Bundled Cascadia Mono WOFF2 subset for Unicode 16 octants fallback
decisions.why: No mainstream OS ships octants by default (Sep 2024). Runtime cascade: octant → sextant → braille → block covers system variance.

audit_meta.naive_version: Draw a sine wave into an offscreen buffer. Walk a 10×10 grid, print . for dark cells and # for bright. Done.
audit_meta.reproducibility: git clone https://github.com/Joona-t/glyph-grid.git cd glyph-grid open index.html  # macOS; start index.html on Windows; xdg-open on Linux # Edit CONFIG object at top of index.html and reload

stack_top3.why: 2D graphics rendering, offscreen Graphics buffers, frame callback.
frame-to-ZIP export in browser for recording mode.
post-render stitch (Bayer dither via palettegen/paletteuse, called from bash).

takeaway: Single-file tools live or die on CONFIG clarity. Second: byte-identity constraints on generative art are real and worth locking in early (v1 compat guard saves you from breaking 8 finalized pieces).
audit_meta.tldr: Single-file p5.js renderer overlaying ASCII character grids on geometric scenes, byte-identical under v1 compat.
tradeoffs: No GPU rendering path initially; error-diffusion dither cannot run on WebGL (no inter-fragment communication). Validator auto-downgrades to Bayer.
Single HTML file means no module system; all CONFIG at top, no layering
Recording mode is deterministic but unoptimized; 4sec × 30fps on high grid density can be slow
v_next: WebGL scene renderer path (skip dither on GPU path)
Runtime UI for CONFIG editing instead of source edit + reload
Octant shape-vector mode (6D shape selection, orthogonal to brightness ramp)
Stream frames to MP4 instead of ZIP (chunked encode)
audit_meta.verification: No formal tests; verification is visual + byte-identity under CONFIG.compat='v1'. Eight frozen pieces must regenerate byte-identically. CR-5 caught under-sampled Gaussian in XDoG. No runtime errors in recording mode (deterministic seed).

core_loop.path: index.html lines 200-400 (the p5.draw function + glyph sampling loop)
core_loop.role: Walk an N×M grid over source Graphics buffer, sample brightness/color per cell, pick character from ramp, render at source hue.

description: Single-file p5.js ASCII/glyph-grid renderer. Inspired by Nous Research's Hermes Agent skill system.
